calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-06-2020 Off Calendar

Notice And Motion To Strike And Or Tax Cost
Off Calendar Dec-24-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Wendy Chau, Esq., T:415.539.6116, for plaintiff/ moving party;
Sabrina Berdux-Cohen, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, T:415-554-3929, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF ABDELKADER ELGAWADY's Motion To Strike And Or Tax Cost: Plaintiff's motion to tax or strike costs is denied.

Under Code of Civ. Proc. 1032, "a prevailing party is entitled as a matter of right to recover costs in any action or proceeding." Plaintiff's request for the court to exercise "discretion" under 1032(a)(4) is misplaced. A prevailing party includes a "defendant in whose favor a dismissal is entered" such as Defendant in this matter, in whose favor judgment was entered on November 20, following its successful motion for summary judgment. (Code Civ. Proc. 1032(a)(4).) A court may not consider the paying party's financial resources or inability to pay. (See Nelson v. Anderson (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 111, 129.) Thus, Defendant is entitled as a matter of right to recover its costs. (See Charton v. Harkey (2016) 247 Cal.App.4th 730, 734 ["The statute providing that a prevailing party is entitled as a matter of right to recover costs provides trial courts with no discretion to deny prevailing party status to a party who meets the statutory definition of a prevailing party."].)

Code of Civ. Proc. 1033.5(a) provides a list of costs that are allowable under 1032. "[I]f the items appearing in a cost bill appear to be proper charges, the burden is on the party seeking to tax costs to show that they were not reasonable or necessary . . . if the items are properly objected to, they are put in issue and the burden of proof is on the party claiming them as costs." (Sanford v. Rasnick (2016) 246 Cal.App.4th 1121, 1128.) The costs requested by Defendant all appear to be proper, as they are all costs provided for in 1033.5.

Plaintiff does not meet his burden to show that any of the $6,846.55 requested is unreasonable or unnecessary. The City is entitled to costs for filing and motion fees. Under Code of Civ. Proc. 1033.5(c)(1), "[c]osts are allowable if incurred, whether or not paid." "Filing fees are therefore costs incurred but not paid, which are recoverable under the general costs statute. Moreover, section 6103.5 specifically prescribes the inclusion of these fees as costs in a judgment; therefore a trial court does not have any discretion to tax them." (Guillemin v. Stein (2002) 104 Cal.App.4th 156, 164.) Jury fees are also an allowable cost under 1033.5(a)(1) and one that Defendant incurred. (See Def.'s Memo of Costs, Attach. 2e.) Plaintiff does not challenge the necessity of reasonableness of the depositions that were taken, but rather the reasonableness of the transcripts and video recording. 1033.5(a)(3)(A) allows for "[t]aking, video recording, and transcribing" a deposition. Thus, Plaintiff does not show that there was anything unreasonable in the $2,877 sought in deposition costs. Next, interpreter fees for a deposition of a party witness is allowed under 1033.5(a)(3)(B) and Plaintiff does not make any showing that $1,150 is an unreasonable cost for that. Further, Plaintiff provides no support for his assertion, and thus does not meet his burden, that the witness fees "were not authorized by the court." (Plf.'s Mt. 6.)

Finally, as to service of process, an allowable cost under 1033.5(a)(4), Plaintiff does not make any showing that the documents were in fact duplicative. In any event, Defendant successfully meets its burden to show the reasonableness of the costs.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice And Motion To Stay Judgement Pending An Appeal
Off Calendar Dec-24-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Wendy Chau, Esq., T:415.539.6116, for plaintiff/ moving party;
Sabrina Berdux-Cohen, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, T:415-554-3929, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The matter is not argued. Therefore, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF ABDELKADER ELGAWADY's Motion To Stay Judgement Pending An Appeal, as follows:

Off calendar. Plaintiff did not serve and file the motion at least 16 court days before the hearing, as required by Code Civ. Proc., 1005(b). The Court notes that although the City's counsel informed Plaintiff's counsel that he would be out of the office from December 20 to January 6 and would be unavailable to attend any motion, and requested that Plaintiff set the motion in late January, Plaintiff's counsel improperly disregarded that request, in violation of the Local Rules and her obligations of civility and professionalism. (See Local Rule 8.2(a)(2) ["Parties should confer with all other parties before scheduling and noticing a hearing."].)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-15-2019 Continued To Dec-09-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion For A Protective Order Preventing Plaintiff From Taking The Deposition Of Department Of Public Works Director Mohammed Nuru
Off Calendar Mar-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Hunter W. Sims III, Esq., Deputy City Atorney CCSF, Telephone 415-554-4259, appearing for Defendant CCSF;
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For A Protective Order Preventing Plaintiff From Taking The Deposition Of Department Of Public Works Director Mohammed Nuru.

Granted. The Court finds that Mohammed Nuru is Director of the Department of Public Works, and is considered a high ranking government official. Under Westly v Sup. Ct. (2004) 125 Cal.App.4th 907 and Nagle v. Sup. Ct. (1994) 28 Cal.App.4th 1465, Mr. Nuru should not be the first witness deposed, as there are undoubtedly lower level witnesses the City could designate to testify about the circumstances of this trip and fall accident. The plaintiff may obtain discovery into this trip and fall accident by deposing the City's person most knowledgeable on the categories designated by plaintiff pursuant to C.C.P. 2025.230.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Charles Geerhart; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Wendy Chau, Esq. 415-539-6116 for Plaintiff.
Susan Kawala, Deputy City Atorney, 415-554-3827 of Office of the City Attorney for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces the basis for the tentative ruling; this is a trip and fall case and there is no legal basis the Court is aware of. Plaintiff argues causes of action in Complaint and cites cases. Defendant argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument changes the tentatvie ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. The Demurrer is overruled as to the first cause of action and the Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to all other claims that have been demurred to. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling, in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and shall include the language that the 1st cause of action is brought under Government Code 815.2.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185, email:ADelgado@sftc.org, Reported. (D302)
